text.title: Video Generation Async API — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: idempotency-key per submit, exponential backoff polling, total-wait cap, download-before-expiry, provider fallback.

rule.statement: Every submit() call MUST include an idempotency key (sha256(prompt + params + provider)). Retries on the same key MUST NOT trigger a new billable job.
rule.rationale: Without idempotency, network retries double-bill the customer. Key-based dedup is the only safe retry primitive on async billable APIs.

rule.statement: Poll loop MUST use exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, capped at 30s). Tight polling (sleep <1s) is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tight polling burns quota and hits 429s. Exponential backoff with jitter is the industry-standard pattern.

rule.statement: Total poll duration MUST cap at 600s (10 min). On timeout, the job MUST be marked failed-timeout and a fallback path triggered.
rule.rationale: Some jobs hang silently. Without a cap, the worker is starved. 10min is enough for normal generation (60-300s).

rule.statement: On SUCCEEDED status, the artefact URL MUST be downloaded to durable storage (S3 / GCS) before the URL expires (provider TTLs 24-72h). Storing the provider URL as the final artefact is forbidden.

rationale.source: provider URL TTL docs; broken-link incidents

rule.rationale: Provider URLs expire. Downstream pipelines built on raw URLs break silently when TTL elapses.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST define a fallback provider. On timeout, permanent failure, or sustained 5xx, the same idempotency-key request retries against fallback. Single-provider deployment is forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Video providers have outages. Without fallback, the media pipeline stalls for hours.
